mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer spending strength and economic expansion, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher short-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) for 5-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS changes are minimal.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.3% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months consistently coincide with positive SPX forward returns, or Rank IC remains negative over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and stable or declining unemployment rates indicates robust job creation without labor market overheating, supporting sustained earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a Rank IC < 0.03 or predicts negative returns in > 55% of cases over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion and corporate profit growth, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days regardless of short-term market noise.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth falling below 1.0% year-over-year signals a supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, which triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when INDPRO YoY growth drops below the 1.0% threshold.
